fv3_sinsg_flux_xi.reference: Harris et al. (2021), GFDL FV3 Technical Memorandum, §3.5, §6 (sub-grid sin(α) upwind selection); Lin & Rood (1997) MWR; src/operators/wind_ops.jl `compute_flux_with_sinsg_xi_arrayop` (imperative reference).
fv3_sinsg_flux_xi.notes: Metric-corrected ξ-direction flux through a UEdge interface. Uses an upwind-blended closed-form rather than branching on sign(vel): flux = ((v + |v|)·sin_pos + (v − |v|)·sin_neg) / 2 · dx · dt, where `sin_pos` is the sub-grid sin(α) on the upwind side for v>0 (interior: position 3 of cell i−1; west boundary: position 1 of cell 1) and `sin_neg` is the upwind side for v<0 (interior: position 1 of cell i; east boundary: position 3 of cell Nc). The interior/boundary lookup is precomputed by the cubed_sphere grid accessor and exposed as the `sin_pos[p,i,j]` / `sin_neg[p,i,j]` arrays at u_edge stagger; the rule reads them as scalar bindings via the accessor.

metric_bindings.sin_pos: sub-grid sin(α) on the upwind side when vel > 0 — precomputed at u_edge by the grid accessor (`_precompute_sinsg_xi`)
metric_bindings.sin_neg: sub-grid sin(α) on the upwind side when vel < 0
metric_bindings.dx: physical edge length at the UEdge interface (`grid.dx[p,i,j]`)
metric_bindings.dt: scalar time-step binding supplied by the rule consumer
